headlines.title: Officials identify man killed by Secret Service after opening fire near White House
headlines.description: The White House was on lockdown after dozens of gunshots were heard outside Saturday evening.

headlines.title: Rep. Thomas Massie warns GOP will be ‘very vulnerable’ in the midterms
headlines.description: Rep. Thomas Massie, R-Ky., warned on “Meet the Press” that the Republican Party will be “very vulnerable” during the midterm elections.

headlines.title: Trump says he won’t ‘rush’ into a deal with Iran, softening hopes of an imminent agreement
headlines.description: President Donald Trump said he won’t “rush into a deal” to end the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran after senior Republican lawmakers warned it could be a “disastrous mistake.”

headlines.title: Reality check: Spencer Pratt seeks to convert online attention to real-life votes in L.A.
headlines.description: The viral, AI-generated video opens with the iconic Hollywood sign on fire and features Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass wearing makeup to look like the Joker.

headlines.title: White House shooting is latest incident in a string of political violence
headlines.description: The shooting at the White House on Saturday evening is just the latest act of political violence carried out in President Donald Trump’s orbit in recent weeks

headlines.title: Ro Khanna calls for ‘term limits’ and ‘expansion’ of the Supreme Court after voting rights ruling
headlines.description: Rep. Ro Khanna (D-Calif.) tells Meet the Press that the Supreme Court's recent decision on the Voting Rights Act is “an assault on the civil rights legacy” and calls for term limits and expansion to “depoliticize” the nation's highest court.

headlines.title: Massie says he’ll ‘stay engaged’ after primary loss, won’t rule out 2028 run
headlines.description: Rep. Thomas Massie (R-Ky.) tells Meet the Press he “won't rule anything out” as he’s asked about his political future after losing his primary to a Trump-backed challenger.

headlines.title: Republicans Express Concern Over Trump’s Deal With Iran
headlines.description: Meet the Press Moderator Kristen Welker joins Hallie Jackson on Sunday TODAY to discuss Republicans raising concern over what’s included in President Donald Trump’s potential peace deal with Iran. "Really witnessing an extraordinary week of divisions between the president and his own party, all raising questions for Republicans about his priorities as we get deeper into this midterm election cycle,” Kristen says.

headlines.title: Absence of USAID likely slowed Ebola detection and response, former officials say
headlines.description: The Trump administration hollowed out USAID last year, but the State Department denied the changes have hampered its response.

headlines.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
headlines.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

headlines.title: Map: How much you pay in federal and state gas taxes
headlines.description: As national gas price averages head toward $5 per gallon, one state has already suspended its gas tax, and now President Donald Trump and his Cabinet are talking about doing the same.

headlines.title: Justice Department deletes press releases on charges against Jan. 6 rioters
headlines.description: The Justice Department has removed press releases detailing the charges against hundreds of individuals who participated in the Jan.
